name: The Emergency Food Assistance Program
description: Free nutritious, high-quality food for anyone in need
requirements: Depends on household income level and state you live in
additionalInfo: Find region for contact and more information: https://www.fns.usda.gov/tefap/program-contacts

name: Meals on Wheels
description: Free nutritious, home-delivered meals to seniors and homebound adults who cannot purchase or prepare their own food
requirements: 60+ years, homebound due to illness, disability, or other reason for having difficulty preparing meals or shopping for groceries and food
additionalInfo: Each county/region has its own service

name: Dartmouth Oncology Nutrition Playlist
description: Free nutrition classes and simple recipes

name: Dartmouth Cancer Center Playlist
description: Free classes on nutrition, research, exercise, etc.

name: stupidcancer.org
description: Country's largest support group for YA/adolescents with cancer. Includes info and links to other organizations for YA cancer patients as well other people's cancer stories

name: The Stupid Cancer Show
description: Real talk for young adults impacted by cancer

name: Out Cooking It E-Book
description: Free 100+ delicious and healthy recipes e-book

name: First Descents
location: North Carolina, New York, Arkansas, Colorado, Montana, Oregon, California
description: Free 1 week outdoor adventure experiences for young adult cancer survivors

name: cookforyourlife.org
description: Free 1000+ evidence-based recipes, hundreds of nutrition and health articles, and how-to cook videos for cancer prevention and control

name: Project Angel Food
description: Free food and deliveries for people facing critical or life-threatening illness
requirements: Diagnosed with life-threatening illness and are unable to independently access healthy food

name: Northern New England Clinical Oncology Society
description: Emergency need grant for cancer patients in Northern NE facing food insecurity, financial toxicity, etc.
requirements: Hematology/oncology patients under active treatment or receiving palliative care services as they approach hospice

name: Mom's Meals
description: Free home-delivered meals on Medicaid or Medicare Advantage plan
requirements: Over 65, disabled, require assistance with grocery shopping and/or preparing meals, on Medicaid plan that offers a paid meal benefit, on a Medicare Advantage plan that offers a paid meal benefit
additionalInfo: Meals are only up to $9.49 if you don't meet requirements

name: Project Purple
location: Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Connecticut, Vermont, Maine, New York, New Jersey, Pennsylvania
description: Meal assistance for pancreatic cancer patients
requirements: Live in one of the states listed, be in active pancreatic cancer treatment, have email/access to computer, understand dietary restrictions, patient is responsible for ordering meals online
additionalInfo: Choice of meals and 2-4 month subscriptions

name: Cancer1Source Meal & Food Assistance Program
description: 14 nutritious home-delivered meals
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment
additionalInfo: Receive gift cards or prepared meals

name: Cancer1Source Genetic & Genomic Testing Program
description: Up to $150 to assist patients who can't afford out-of-pocket expenses or genetic/genomic testing or genetic counseling
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment

name: Cancer1Source Lymphedema Garment Assistance Program
description: Assist with out-of-pocket costs related to lymphedema treatment
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment

name: Cancer1Source Medication
description: Provide Drug Discount Card to save up to 80% on prescription medicine for drugs not covered by insurance, is a high drug deductible, a low medicine cap that has been met, card offers better price than copay, you are in Medicare Part D donut hole
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment

name: Cancer1Source Transportation Assistance Program
description: Provide limited financial assistance for treatment-related transportation for cancer patients
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment
additionalInfo: Up to $150 per person per year -> gas gift card for personal gas or generic gift card for any type of transportation costs

name: Cancer1Source Wig/Hairpiece Assistance Program
description: Up to $150 of assistance for wigs or hairpieces
requirements: Massachusetts Resident, household income below 400% of federal poverty level, receiving cancer treatment

name: Lasagna Love
description: Free home-delivered lasagna
requirements: Any kind of need

name: New Hampshire Food Bank
description: Provide food for New Hampshire residents who are food insecure
requirements: In need of food

name: Amazon EBT Program
description: Reduced food prices for SNAP and EBT
requirements: SNAP or EBT card holder

name: Cancer Nutrition Consortium
description: Filterable, unique nutritional recipes for those undergoing cancer treatment as well as cancer nutrition awareness

name: Cancer Support Community
location: Montana, Missouri, Kansas, Iowa, Illinois, Minnesota, Wisconsin, Tennessee
description: Offers free programs, support, and services for cancer patients/survivors/caregivers/etc. and information sources such as blogs
requirements: Cancer survivors, patients, caregivers, family, loved ones

name: Why Hunger
description: Connects people to free, nutritious food
requirements: Based on financial need

name: SNAP
description: Offers free help buying groceries
requirements: Based on financial need

name: Abbott Nutrition Pathway Plus Program
description: Provides free supplement products to eligible cancer patients facing financial issues
requirements: Based on lack of insurance coverage and financial need

name: Maribo Cares Food Program
description: Free food boxes to seniors aged 60+ in US undergoing cancer treatment
requirements: 60+ undergoing cancer treatment

name: Wellness House Online Exercise Programs
description: Free online yoga, mind/body movement, Pilates, Tai-Chi, etc. for cancer patients
requirements: Cancer patients of any abilities and experiences

name: Hope Connections Online Fitness Classes
description: Free virtual online fitness, support, and educational classes for cancer patients and caregivers 18+ in USA
requirements: Cancer patients/caregivers 18+ in US

name: Lending Hearts Virtual Programs
description: Free virtual wellness programs for cancer patients in the US
requirements: Cancer patients in US

name: Cancer Pathways Online Wellness/Community Class
description: Free online yoga, art, and wellness/support classes for cancer patients
requirements: For cancer patients and their loved ones

name: Moving for Life Online Exercise Program
description: Free virtual exercise program for cancer patients anywhere in the USA
requirements: Based on income

name: LivingWell Yoga Program
description: Free weekly online classes with instructors as well as youtube playlist

name: Life with Cancer
description: Free virtual (& in-person) classes about yoga, mindfulness, stress management, health etc.

name: Blood Cancer United Dietitian Consultation
description: Free nutrition consultations via phone or email with an experienced registered oncology dietitian for nutrition education and side-effect management for all cancer patients in the USA
requirements: All cancer patients in the USA

name: chemocare.com
description: Comprehensive source of information about chemotherapy drugs, side effect informations, cancer wellness information, etc. for cancer patients and their caregivers

name: Chemocessories
description: Provide free set of accessories (ex: scarves, jewelry, etc.) for women going through cancer treatment and hair loss
requirements: Recipients must be undergoing cancer treatment or have completed treatment within the last 12 months in the US

name: EBeauty
description: Provide free wigs to women to restore self-image and confidence during cancer treatments
requirements: Women in the US undergoing cancer treatment

name: Hair We Share
description: Provides free wigs for <18 or free wig based on financial need for 18+
requirements: US residents with permanent hair loss due to a medical condition (cancer, alopecia, trauma, etc.)

name: Heavenly Hats
description: Provides brand-new hats for patients who have lost hair due to a medical condition to improve self-image and courage
requirements: Cancer/medical patients who lost their hair due to a disease or the treatment of a disease

name: Hope Scarves
description: Provides free headscarves paired with survivor stories to offer encouragement to those in active treatment
requirements: Cancer survivors in active treatment throughout the world

name: Locks of Love
description: Provide high quality hair prosthetics for children and young adults up to 21 suffering from hair loss to boost self confidence
requirements: Children and young adults up to 21 in U.S. and Canada

name: Look Good Feel Better
description: Teach beauty techniques to people with cancer to give them a sense of confidence and community
requirements: Cancer patients worldwide

name: Look Good Feel Better for Teens
description: Offers free materials and information to help teens deal with the effects of cancer treatment
requirements: Teens 13 to 17 dealing with cancer

name: Wigs and Wishes
description: Provides free wigs and grants wishes to courageous individuals battling cancer
requirements: Women and children with cancer

name: Wigs for Kids (WFK)
description: Free hair replacement systems and support for children who have lost their hair due to medical issues worldwide
requirements: Patients under 18 worldwide

name: American Cancer Society Road to Recovery
description: Free ride to cancer-related medical appointment
requirements: Cancer-related medical appointment

name: American Cancer Society Patient Lodging Programs
location: Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Iowa, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maryland, Massachusetts, Minnesota, Mississippi, Missouri, Nebraska, New York, North Carolina, Ohio, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Vermont
description: Offer people facing cancer and their caregivers a home while receiving cancer treatment
requirements: For patients needing housing accommodations for cancer treatment

name: ACS Cares
description: A free mobile app that provides cancer patients and loved ones resources to navigate their journey such as personalized cancer related information and connection with volunteers
requirements: For people with cancer, their families, and their caregivers

name: American Cancer Society Live Chat or call
description: 24/7 everyday call line to get assistance with cancer-related information and other inquiries
requirements: For cancer patients, caregivers, and family members

name: Courage for the Soul Head Scarves
description: Provides free head scarves for cancer patients in the US who are experiencing hair loss
requirements: For cancer patients in active treatment in the USA who are experiencing hair loss

name: Knots of Love
description: Provides free handmade beanies to cancer patients in the USA
requirements: For cancer patients in the USA undergoing chemotherapy treatment

name: The Turban Project
description: Provides free beautiful handmade head coverings to cancer patients nationwide including turban, cap, beanie, or crochet hat
requirements: For cancer patients in the USA

name: The Pink Wig Project
description: Provides free fun, pink wigs for cancer patients in the USA in active treatment
requirements: Cancer patients in the USA who are in active treatment

name: Pink Heart Funds Free Wig Program
description: Provides wigs for those who are uninsured or underinsured in the MS Coast counties who are experiencing hair loss due to cancer or extreme balding disorders
requirements: Hair loss patients who are uninsured or underinsured in MS Local Coast counties
additionalInfo: Hancock, Harrison, Jackson, Pearl River, Stone, and George Counties in MS Gulf Coast

name: Pink Heart Funds Breast Prosthetic Program
description: Provides breast prosthesis and bras to uninsured or underinsured women in the USA
requirements: Hair loss patients who are uninsured or underinsured in MS Local Coast counties
additionalInfo: Hancock, Harrison, Jackson, Pearl River, Stone, and George Counties in MS Gulf Coast

name: Angel Hair Inc. Free Wig Program
description: One free wig every 5 years to cancer patients who have experienced hair loss due to treatments
requirements: Cancer patients in the USA who have experienced hair loss due to treatments

name: National Wigs for Cancer Society
description: Free wig program for both insured and uninsured cancer patients in the USA
requirements: For cancer patients in the USA

name: TRS Care Wig Program
description: Helps insured cancer patients in the USA qualify for free medical wigs and post-mastectomy essentials through insurance
requirements: For insured cancer patients in the USA

name: Battle Cap Project
description: Soft, unique, free handmade caps to cancer patients anywhere in the world
requirements: For cancer patients anywhere in the world

name: The Butterfly Club
description: National free wig program for people who experience hair loss due to cancer treatments
requirements: For cancer patients in the USA

name: Verma Foundation
description: Provides free cap wig to <18 and for >18 based on financial need
requirements: For cancer patients
additionalInfo: Can also bypass application and purchase cap wigs
